course_title: Tops in Ital Lit in Trans: Dante's Inferno
subject: ITAL
course_description: Topics to be announced each semester. Course taught in English. May be repeated for credit when syllabus changes. Focus on developing critical analysis and reasoning skills.
course_notes: (Same as CL 329R 2) (Complete Topic: Dante's Inferno in English Translation) (In this class, we will read Dante's Inferno, the first part of the Divine Comedy, in English translation, and explore its historical context in Medieval Europe. Besides reading excerpts from Dante's classic sources, such as Virgil and Ovid, we will look at echoes of the Commedia in modern literature and culture, exploring, in particular, how the Inferno has been used to think about and represent the modern metropolis and the expansion of capitalist society. While studying Dante's worldview and the geographical construction of mental space, we will follow the translation of his work across different media and genres in contemporary reception, from theater to the novel.)
